blocks.content: Shirone was not in a good mood.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: An unprovoked attack?
Honestly, it was a chilling aura, and the destructive power contained within it must have been beyond imagination.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The ship almost sank.
It was fortunate that he suppressed it before the battle started; otherwise, the passengers' lives would have been in danger.
Shirone's unusual display of killing intent was partly due to his anger over this.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Foreign Minister Cairns.
Although he knew roughly through Omega, Cairns was quite young.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The records I remember of Cairns are up to when he was twelve. Of course, he wasn't ordinary even then.
He couldn't even imagine how he had evolved over the next 22 years.
Shirone massaged his eyes.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I'm tired.
He had no intention of getting deeply involved in politics, but Langi was on his mind.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: He called her an object. Referring to a person.
Even during the World Beauty Pageant, she entertained high-ranking officials, so it wasn't strange in itself.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: However, the smile she showed at the end was genuine, without a trace of falsehood.
Isn't that reason enough to get involved in politics?
Arachne's sailing ship.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I'm sorry.
In a large room decorated like a palace, Cairns was drinking with dancers.

blocks.speaker: Benahar
blocks.content: Do you even know how to say that?
Among the dancers with friendly smiles, Cairns' eyes were as cold as ice.
Benahar bowed his head again.

blocks.speaker: Benahar
blocks.content: I was mistaken. Shirone's prowess in person is beyond imagination.

blocks.speaker: Benahar
blocks.content: How do you know? You didn't even fight him.
Half of it was true.
If a battle had actually taken place, it might have been a good fight, but now it was meaningless.
His spirit is broken. That man's mind… is not human.
No matter how many life-threatening situations, compressing a human's lifetime into war, it was an impossible level of mental strength.
Regardless of skill level, those who could 'fight' Shirone were mentally trained to an extraordinary degree.
Trained? No, insane. Only in the realm of madness could one confront Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Benahar
blocks.content: Anyway, it's fine.

blocks.speaker: Benahar
blocks.content: We'll just have to wait for the right time. You can go back now. And call Langi.

blocks.speaker: Benahar
blocks.content: Well…
Several facts collided in Benahar's mind, making him speak with a troubled expression.

blocks.speaker: Benahar
blocks.content: Things got a bit complicated.
Cairns, who had been looking at Benahar with one eyebrow raised, gave a meaningless sneer.
That evening.
Shirone left the work to Poine and headed alone to Arachne's sailing ship.
Flying through the air, he easily crossed the 700-meter distance and saw the deck where a banquet was taking place.
They were all officials, and a feast of delicacies that couldn't be found on other ships continued.
Landing among the people drinking under the cozy moonlight in the windless zone, Shirone sighed.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Phew.
They weren't surprised by the magic.
They were just displeased by the fact that he had broken the mutual treaty and invaded the ship through the air.

blocks.speaker: Deputy Minister
blocks.content: What are you? Do you know where you are…
As a man with a medal tried to move, a guard magician stopped him.

blocks.speaker: Guard
blocks.content: Deputy Minister, that's Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Deputy Minister
blocks.content: The Five Great Stars?
Though his abilities were far inferior to Cairns, the deputy minister had the weapon of experience.

blocks.speaker: Deputy Minister
blocks.content: Hmm, I see the star of the Ivory Tower.
Even if he was a thorn in the side of politicians, there was no benefit in antagonizing the Ivory Tower.
At least not until the Holy War began.

blocks.speaker: Deputy Minister
blocks.content: I came to see Cairns. Where is he now?
A young man calling the minister by name?
He wasn't one to be provoked by trivial matters, but he had to address official business.

blocks.speaker: Deputy Minister
blocks.content: He's not attending the banquet, so he must be in his quarters. But what is this about?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I have a few questions. If you won't guide me, step aside.
Phew.
As the wine he had enjoyed rose, the deputy minister exhaled deeply, releasing his intoxication.

blocks.speaker: Deputy Minister
blocks.content: Well, fine. I shouldn't interfere with the star of the Ivory Tower's business. But as I understand it, the maritime exchange was first proposed by you, Shirone. And Arachne gladly accepted. But now…

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Who ordered the banquet?

blocks.speaker: Deputy Minister
blocks.content: The agreement on the inventory check is concluded. Do you have a problem with consuming our own food?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: On the deck?

blocks.speaker: Deputy Minister
blocks.content: The weather is nice, and the moon is bright.
As Shirone's eyes hardened, he straightened his posture and continued.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I understand what you mean. Other ships can't eat as abundantly. But we are an elite force on a national mission. We have our routines and clear goals. This kind of emotional approach doesn't excuse breaking the treaty.
As expected of the deputy minister of foreign affairs, his psychological warfare and eloquence were strong.

blocks.speaker: Deputy Minister
blocks.content: A treaty.
Thinking of what Benahar had done earlier that day, Shirone was exasperated.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Fine. I apologize for breaking the treaty.
The nobles murmured.

blocks.speaker: Deputy Minister
blocks.content: Well, no.
The deputy minister tried to suppress a smile, but his shoulders tensed.

blocks.speaker: Deputy Minister
blocks.content: This minor issue, right? The relationship between the Ivory Tower and Arachne isn't…
Seeing Shirone's gaze, the deputy minister's heart sank.

blocks.speaker: Deputy Minister
blocks.content: Then let's hear from the minister how well the treaty is being kept. There shouldn't be any serious issues, right? But if there's a severe breach of trust…
The nobles couldn't even blink.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Don't expect this ship to be afloat by tomorrow morning.
Turning to leave, Shirone saw the deputy minister's hammer-struck expression and extended his hand.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No, wait. Star, no, Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Enjoy the banquet.
Shirone turned his head and scanned the people.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Don't let anyone leave. Stay here until I return to avoid any trouble.
Benahar came out, opening the door to the quarters.

blocks.speaker: Benahar
blocks.content: What's going on?
The moment he saw Shirone, memories of the events from earlier in the day came flooding back, causing him to break out in a cold sweat.

blocks.speaker: Benahar
blocks.content: Oh, Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: As I mentioned earlier, I'm here to see the minister. I heard he's in his quarters.
Benahar glanced at the deputy minister behind Shirone.
'No, no!'
The deputy minister silently mouthed words while making a throat-slitting gesture with his hand.
'Don't let him see the minister. Make up any excuse—illness, anything!'

blocks.speaker: Benahar
blocks.content: Come, I'll guide you.
The deputy minister, knowing Benahar's personality, looked at him in disbelief but couldn't meet his eyes.
As the door closed, the remaining nobles felt a chill despite the warm southern climate.

blocks.speaker: Benahar
blocks.content: ...Damn it.

blocks.speaker: Benahar
blocks.content: Please forgive what happened earlier. I didn't recognize who you were and acted rashly.

blocks.speaker: Benahar
blocks.content: But as a magician, he also held a certain respect for Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Benahar
blocks.content: Someone could have been hurt. If you have a problem with me, call me separately next time.

blocks.speaker: Benahar
blocks.content: ...That won't happen again.
When the door opened, Cairns and Langi were sitting side by side at a table in a luxurious dining room.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Langi?
Shirone asked, but Benahar just bowed and held the door handle.

blocks.speaker: Benahar
blocks.content: Have a good time.
As Shirone approached, Cairns stood up.

blocks.speaker: Cairns
blocks.content: Welcome. It's an honor to meet the star of the Ivory Tower. As the rumors say, quite a handsome man…

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Langi, what's going on?
Shirone didn't intend to ignore Cairns, but it was clear that calling Langi wasn't out of goodwill.

blocks.speaker: Cairns
blocks.content: Haha! You mentioned having a prior engagement. Since Langi and I aren't strangers, I thought it would be nice to have her join us. I apologize if it upset you.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone…
Langi looked more beautiful than usual, but her heart's window was already closed.

blocks.speaker: Langi
blocks.content: Let's hear what he has to say first.
As Shirone took a seat opposite Cairns, staff brought in food as if they had been waiting.

blocks.speaker: Cairns
blocks.content: There's a banquet outside…

blocks.speaker: Cairns
blocks.content: I apologize!
Cairns placed his hands on the table and bowed, causing the staff to freeze.

blocks.speaker: Cairns
blocks.content: It's all my fault. I apologize for what happened earlier, the banquet, and breaking the treaty. I'll take full responsibility.
It was clear he understood Shirone's intentions without being told, which was impressive, but…

blocks.speaker: Cairns
blocks.content: How will you take responsibility?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Words are meaningless without action.

blocks.speaker: Cairns
blocks.content: We'll share our food. We're also guests here, so we should share. We'll agree to an inventory check. You can use our forces for security if needed.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Hmm.
Shirone crossed his legs.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What is Arachne aiming for in this voyage?

blocks.speaker: Cairns
blocks.content: I'll be honest. As you know, Dionas is a nation founded by pirates. The king rules with an iron fist, and most of the population survives through maritime plunder. We want to officially recognize Dionas as a state of Cartesia in exchange for one thing.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: And that one thing is?

blocks.speaker: Cairns
blocks.content: Artifacts from the ancient civilization of Maika.
The moment he finished speaking, Shirone felt a chill.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What is that…
When he searched the 5D cube containing all atomic movements since the beginning of time,

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What?

blocks.speaker: Cairns
blocks.content: There was no record.

blocks.speaker: Cairns
blocks.content: I don't know the details. No one does. It's a civilization that existed somewhere in the South Pacific long ago and is believed to be submerged now.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: And Dionas knows about it?

blocks.speaker: Cairns
blocks.content: Cartesia is a remote part of the world, so it might be unfamiliar to you. But the Ten Kingdoms of the Archipelago have been investigating Maika's artifacts for a long time.

blocks.speaker: Cairns
blocks.content: However… since the world war, Dionas has been suspicious. Even if they weren't affected by tsunamis, it's strange that they haven't suffered from demon attacks either.
How should he take this?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: 'If it's not in Omega, it doesn't exist. Therefore, the Maika civilization is just a myth.'

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: …he thought, but then, 'But even myths should be recorded. Why don't I know about it?'

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If the general public knew, but Shirone didn't, it meant it was removed only from Omega.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: 'Erased? No, that's impossible. Even if reset, the erasure log remains.'

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It wasn't a logical deduction.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: 'The outside world.'

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The only thing left after removing all categories existing in Omega.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: 'It's somehow related to the outside world. Otherwise, it couldn't bypass Omega.'

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Having thought this far, Shirone asked,

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Even if Dionas found artifacts or traces, would they share them?

blocks.speaker: Cairns
blocks.content: That's why I'm going. Armed with immense treasures, weapons, and the most beautiful women.

blocks.speaker: Cairns
blocks.content: Things that would come out in an inventory check.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: You think that's enough to persuade them? Even if they're pirates, isn't that underestimating them?

blocks.speaker: Cairns
blocks.content: That might be true. But what if I told you that King Kalt of Dionas…

blocks.speaker: Cairns
blocks.content: Is obsessed with Miss Arachne? To the point of attempting an armed invasion.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: After all, they were pirates.

blocks.speaker: Cairns
blocks.content: Of course, Langi is a valuable asset. But with the Holy War approaching, we must do our best.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: 'This is why I didn't want to get involved in politics.'

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: To achieve what you want, you have to see many things you don't want to.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Let go. Langi is my friend too. I won't forgive you for doing that in front of me.

blocks.speaker: Cairns
blocks.content: She's still mine for now.

blocks.speaker: Cairns
blocks.content: Cairns, pressing closer to Langi, spoke with a challenging look.

blocks.speaker: Cairns
blocks.content: She's necessary for negotiations. But… if the Five Great Stars help, we might not need her.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: He thought it was over.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: 'Nothing is impossible in this world. This way, even the Five Great Stars will come under me.'

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Cairns.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Have you ever heard of the name Fermi?

blocks.speaker: Cairns
blocks.content: No, I haven't.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: You have a lot to learn from him.
